sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the positions:
- Yellow block: rows 20-21, cols 24-25 (top container)
- Blue dots: scattered at rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (top container)
- Red block: rows 30-33, cols 12-17 (middle row, left side)
- Dark red block: rows 30-33, cols 18-21 (middle row, right side)
- Blue block: rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (bottom container)
- Green center: rows 40-41, cols 10-11 (bottom container)
The blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, which is above the green center at rows 40-41, cols 10-11. The blue block overlaps with the green center's columns (8-11 vs 10-11), so it's blocking the path.
I need to move the blue block out of the way. Let me click the blue target to swap the blue block and blue dots. Then the blue block will be at rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (top container), and the blue dots will be at rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (bottom container).
Wait, but the blue dots are scattered 1-pixel objects, and the blue block is a 16-pixel object. When I click the blue target, they swap positions. So the blue block (16 px) will move to rows 20-23, cols 18-21, and the blue dots (8 px) will move to rows 34-37, cols 8-11.
But the blue dots at rows 34-37, cols 8-11 would still be in the bottom container, potentially blocking the green center's path.
Actually, looking at the image, the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, which is a 4x4 block. The green center is at rows 40-41, cols 10-11, which is a 2x2 block. The blue block is above the green center, so it's blocking the path up.
If I click the blue target, the blue block will move to rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (top container), and the blue dots will move to rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (bottom container). The blue dots are scattered 1-pixel objects, so they might not block the path as effectively as the blue block.
Let me try clicking the blue target, then moving the green center up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click blue target to swap blue block and blue dots
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
